violations.text: Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of black/brown debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2026

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 02/06/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice was voluntarily disposed of (see attached disposal form). Ice machine was taken out of service and must not be used until it is cleaned. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/23/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 09/14/2023

Eggs and cream cheese were observed held at 44-50 degrees Fahrenheit. In the ice well at the buffet line. Items were observed in the ice well with only half of the container of the food in ice. Per PIC, food was placed in the ice wells 2.5 hours prior to inspection and moved to refrigeration to rapidly cool. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/14/2023
The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine has an accumulation of biofilm. New Violation. Correct By: 09/14/2023
